title: Xautoで以下の投稿についてX投稿文に作成せよ。https://sakana.ai/mic-project/
summary_text: 2026年4月7日公開の一次情報ベースで、`x-auto` 向けに本文URLなしの英語ドラフトを作りました。`source_url` には [Sakana AIの記事](https://sakana.ai/mic-project/) を入れる前提です。分類は `P2 / 速報解説+切込み型` が自然です。 ```text “Misinformation defense is becoming an engineering stack.” Sakana AI announced on April 7, 2026 that it completed a system for Japan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications project on countermeasures against online misinformation and disinformation in FY2025. The important part is not the announcement itself. It is the architecture. They split the problem into 3 layers. First, map the SNS space by narratives, not by raw impressions. Their novelty search recursively samples X posts and organizes them into a hierarchical narrative tree, so teams can see which lines of discourse actually matter. Second, verify with multiple detectors, not one model. They combine frontier foundation models, Sakana’s own detectors, reverse image search, claim extraction, web and SNS search, and evidence-linked fact checking with explicit reasoning. Third, simulate intervention before acting. Using their ABM framework “Shachi,” they model personas and test whether counter-messaging will actually reduce spread. This is the shift many teams still miss. Disinformation response is not a single-classifier problem. It is a full pipeline: discover, verify, explain, then simulate the countermeasure. If you build AI for high-stakes domains such as healthcare, governance, or public communication, this design matters. ``` 必要ならこのまま `title / pillar / category / pattern / source_url` まで Notion登録用の形に整えます。
